subgraph.label: Subgraph
subgraph.description: Reusable child graph surfaced through wrapper nodes and child tabs.

agent.label: Agent
agent.description: Legacy/internal higher-level artifact family retained for compatibility metadata, not a separate visible runtime.

deep_agent.label: Deep Agent
deep_agent.description: Legacy/internal future-facing family aliased into the current LangGraph trunk.

langgraph_sync.label: LangGraph Sync
langgraph_sync.description: Visible synchronous execution profile for the current LangGraph trunk.

langgraph_async.label: LangGraph Async
langgraph_async.description: Visible asynchronous execution profile for the current LangGraph trunk.

langchain_agent.label: LangChain agent shell
langchain_agent.description: Advanced LangChain-flavored agent-shell profile used for editor/export surfaces.

deepagents.label: DeepAgents runtime mode
deepagents.description: Advanced DeepAgents-flavored profile with values-first streaming defaults, still trunk-backed.

langgraph.label: LangGraph mode
langgraph.description: Primary graph/subgraph builder and runtime mode.

langchain.label: LangChain mode
langchain.description: Advanced agent-shell authoring mode with its own node/API surface. Editor-first for now; not an in-app runtime mode.

deepagents.label: DeepAgents mode
deepagents.description: Advanced DeepAgents-flavored mode with its own node/API surface and a values-first streaming runtime style, still backed by the current trunk.

uiAbstractionNotes: The tools badge/chip is a graphical abstraction. Linking tools can compile into an explicit ToolNode loop or auto-plumbed tool execution.
Tools remain bounded to the subset explicitly linked by the circuit author. A capable model may choose among that linked set, but it does not gain global tool access.
Structured output stays on the node as authored intent, even when the compiled graph stores the parsed payload in state/custom_vars.
linkSemantics: Linking tools provisions an authored tool subset for this node; runtime choice, when supported, stays bounded to that linked subset.
A direct edge can still coexist with tool links; the compile path may replace the literal edge with a conditional tool loop when tools are linked.
The tools handle is often many-to-one at authoring time even if the compiled graph inserts one ToolNode.
linkMultiplicity: Message/document/state ports behave like ordinary directed graph links.
The tools handle is many-to-one at authoring time: several tool blocks can feed one authored node.
A direct outgoing edge can coexist with tool links even though compile may synthesize a ToolNode loop.
llm_chat.compiledGraphRelation: Authoring intent may compile into the authored node plus an explicit or implicit ToolNode loop, with structured output written back into graph state rather than emitted as a literal separate node.
debugProjection: tool links are surfaced as authored semantics
structured output is projected as state/custom_vars writes
runtime logs may mention the synthesized tool loop rather than a literal tools chip

react_agent.summary: Agent-flavored abstraction still compiled through the current LangGraph trunk.
uiAbstractionNotes: The tools badge/chip is a graphical abstraction. Linking tools can compile into an explicit ToolNode loop or auto-plumbed tool execution.
Tools remain bounded to the subset explicitly linked by the circuit author. A capable model may choose among that linked set, but it does not gain global tool access.
Structured output stays on the node as authored intent, even when the compiled graph stores the parsed payload in state/custom_vars.
linkSemantics: Linking tools provisions an authored tool subset for this node; runtime choice, when supported, stays bounded to that linked subset.
A direct edge can still coexist with tool links; the compile path may replace the literal edge with a conditional tool loop when tools are linked.
The tools handle is often many-to-one at authoring time even if the compiled graph inserts one ToolNode.
linkMultiplicity: Message/document/state ports behave like ordinary directed graph links.
The tools handle is many-to-one at authoring time: several tool blocks can feed one authored node.
A direct outgoing edge can coexist with tool links even though compile may synthesize a ToolNode loop.
react_agent.compiledGraphRelation: Authoring intent may compile into the authored node plus an explicit or implicit ToolNode loop, with agent/tool semantics preserved in runtime metadata instead of requiring one literal graph edge per internal step.
debugProjection: tool links are surfaced as authored semantics
runtime may project synthesized tool-loop events
structured output is projected through state/custom_vars rather than a separate visible node

sub_agent.summary: Saved or wrapped LangChain agent artifact surface. Use this when you want to reference a richer agent artifact, not the canonical agent-as-tool subagent model.
uiAbstractionNotes: This block references a saved LangChain agent artifact or wrapper-backed agent unit.
Use tool_sub_agent for the canonical subagent-as-tool model. Use subgraph_node for graph-native child graphs.
linkSemantics: One visual node can stand for a lowered LangChain bridge, an embedded-native agent artifact, or another bounded LangChain-derived subagent form depending on metadata.
Some references reopen a source LangChain artifact instead of exposing internal compiled structure.
linkMultiplicity: One visual node can represent a LangChain-authored agent/subagent artifact rather than every internal step of that agent.
Its direct edges are authored as graph links even when runtime executes an embedded or lowered agent artifact under the trunk.
sub_agent.compiledGraphRelation: Compiled shape depends on execution metadata: lowered LangChain bridge wrapper or embedded-native agent artifact wrapper. This node is intentionally not the graph-native child-subgraph surface.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should identify the source LangChain artifact or subagent wrapper contract
reopen flow points back to the authored LangChain artifact rather than exposing every internal compiled step

rag_retriever_local.summary: Local vector retrieval against a persisted Chroma store.

tool_sub_agent.summary: Canonical subagent surface: a LangChain-style agent callable as a tool, configured in the project Subagent Library and invoked ephemerally by a parent agent.
uiAbstractionNotes: A subagent is an agent used as a tool by a parent agent. It is configured in the Subagent Library, then referenced by this block.
Subagents do not imply independent persistent memory by default; they are invoked ephemerally per call.
tool_sub_agent.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a callable LangChain-style subagent tool wrapper using a project-level Subagent Library definition.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should identify the selected subagent library entry and the parent agent invoking it

tool_llm_worker.summary: Specialist worker tool surface. Useful for delegated tasks without implying a full separate agent runtime.

memory_store_read.summary: Cross-thread store/profile read in the current runtime; use this for user/profile-scoped long-lived store access rather than lightweight helper reads.
linkSemantics: One state input can drive a store lookup whose user/profile scope is derived from configured state keys.
The output is a memory payload written back into graph state, not a direct inspection of checkpoint internals.
uiAbstractionNotes: This is long-lived store/profile access, not just peeking at the current step state.
The compiled node performs a store lookup even though the canvas only shows one simple memory block.
linkMultiplicity: Usually one state input to one memory payload output; the real namespace/user resolution happens through params and runtime context.
memory_store_read.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store-backed lookup keyed by namespace + user identity rather than a direct edge-level memory graph.
debugProjection: state/debug surfaces should identify namespace/user-derived lookup results when available

memoryreader.summary: Lightweight helper read surface. In the current build it still resolves through the runtime store rather than peeking raw checkpoint/thread state.
linkSemantics: A single state input can trigger one memory read result written back into state.
Although it looks like a lightweight state helper, it currently compiles against the runtime store.
uiAbstractionNotes: This surface is visually lighter than memory_store_read but still goes through store access in the current build.
That distinction must remain visible to avoid confusing thread state with long-lived store state.
memoryreader.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store read against a fixed memory namespace/key pattern rather than a pure in-memory state peek.
debugProjection: debug/state should treat returned memory data as store-derived, not raw checkpoint state

memorywriter.summary: Lightweight helper write surface. In the current build it still persists through the runtime store rather than mutating checkpoint/thread state directly.
linkSemantics: A single input path can persist one selected state value as a memory item.
The canvas does not show the namespace/key plumbing explicitly even though the compiled node writes into the runtime store.
uiAbstractionNotes: This surface looks lightweight but currently writes through the store layer.
Users should not confuse it with plain in-thread state mutation.
memorywriter.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store write into a fixed memory namespace/key pattern rather than a raw custom_vars update.
debugProjection: debug/state should identify persisted memory writes separately from ordinary state updates

deep_agent_suite.summary: Advanced DeepAgents-flavored suite surface. Currently adapter-backed and trunk-compiled rather than a fully separate runtime family.
uiAbstractionNotes: This surface is a product-level suite abstraction, not a separate native runtime graph in the current build.
Opening or inspecting it should explain clearly that current compilation routes through trunk-compatible surfaces.
linkSemantics: Edges from this node remain ordinary graph edges even though the surface is suite-flavored.
The visual node can out-express the currently compiled runtime shape.
linkMultiplicity: Can accept ordinary graph edges and tool semantics while still compiling as an adapter-backed alias.
The visual suite node is broader than the current compiled representation.
deep_agent_suite.compiledGraphRelation: Currently compiles as an alias-backed trunk surface rather than a fully separate DeepAgents runtime graph.
debugProjection: runtime/debug output should identify the suite surface as adapter-backed
the visual suite node does not imply a second root runtime

deep_subagent_worker.summary: Advanced DeepAgents worker surface currently compiled as an adapter-backed specialist tool.

deep_memory_skill.summary: Legacy DeepAgents memory skill mapped onto the canonical store read.

user_input_node.summary: Bootstrap input node that seeds graph state from user or test payloads.

debug_print.summary: Debug helper that mirrors selected state into run/debug surfaces.

static_text.summary: Static text/value emitter for prompts, labels, and deterministic payloads.

python_executor_node.summary: Advanced free-code Python block. Powerful, environment-dependent, and less portable than structured nodes.
linkSemantics: Graph edges only describe data-flow shape; the internal semantics are defined by arbitrary Python code.
One simple visual node can therefore hide multiple side effects, derived values, or control decisions.
uiAbstractionNotes: This is intentionally a free-code escape hatch.
It is more powerful but less portable, less inspectable, and less statically understandable than structured nodes.
linkMultiplicity: The node presents one compact graph shape even though the code can implement richer internal logic.
python_executor_node.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to an exec-based Python block; the graph only constrains input/output attachment, not internal behavior.
debugProjection: run/debug output may include stdout-like or custom result payloads rather than strongly typed structured events

data_container.summary: Structured variable container that seeds or merges custom state values.

chat_output.summary: Terminal chat/message output surface for graph or agent responses.

tool_executor.summary: Explicit ToolNode surface. Useful when you want tool execution shown as a first-class graph step rather than auto-inserted plumbing.
uiAbstractionNotes: This surface exists because some users want tool execution visible as a graph step even though the compiler can auto-insert a ToolNode loop from linked tools.
Use this when the circuit author wants tool execution represented as an explicit workflow step instead of delegating bounded choice to a tool-capable model node.
linkSemantics: May be used explicitly or synthesized implicitly from linked tools on agent/llm nodes.
linkMultiplicity: May appear as one explicit graph step, or be synthesized from many tool links on an agent-like node.
The same authored intent can therefore have a literal node or only runtime/tool-loop projection depending on compile strategy.
tool_executor.compiledGraphRelation: Can map 1:1 to a literal ToolNode or be omitted from authoring entirely and synthesized by compilation from linked tools.
debugProjection: run/debug surfaces may show explicit ToolNode execution even when the authoring surface used only tool chips/links

logic_router.summary: Conditional router reading a state field and choosing the next labeled edge.

context_trimmer.summary: Context/history trimming helper for message-heavy workflows.

parallel_aggregator.summary: Parallel fan-in helper that merges several state inputs into one output key.

human_interrupt.summary: Human-in-the-loop interrupt marker compiled through the runtime checkpoint flow.

memory_checkpoint.summary: Checkpoint marker that enables pause/resume and per-thread persistence behavior.
debugProjection: graph scope marker
checkpoint enabled at compile time
checkpoint snapshots available per step when a checkpointer is configured
uiAbstractionNotes: This surface does not represent a literal runtime node step.
It acts as a graph-scope marker that enables checkpointing/persistence at compile time.
It may remain detached on the canvas by design; that does not invalidate the graph.
memory_checkpoint.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a graph-level checkpointer configuration rather than a traversed node in the runtime graph.
memory_checkpoint.graphScopeExplanation: Detached by design: this marker enables graph-level checkpointing when the graph is compiled. It is not a step-local state-capture node.

update_state_node.summary: Direct state mutation helper for targeted key updates.

file_loader_node.summary: Document/file loader that pushes structured content into graph state.

tool_python_repl.summary: Advanced Python REPL tool surface. Powerful but environment-dependent and non-portable across hosts.

tool_python_function.summary: Advanced inline Python function tool surface. Powerful but environment-dependent and non-portable across hosts.

tool_web_search.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the Tavily-backed web_search runtime tool.

tool_rest_api.summary: Structured REST API tool surface for bounded external calls.

tool_api_call.summary: Generic API call tool surface for explicitly configured integrations.

tool_sql_query.summary: Read-only SQL query tool surface. Mutation requests are blocked by preflight in this pass.
tool_sql_query.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the sql_query runtime tool with read-only-first semantics.

tool_rpg_dice_roller.summary: Deterministic utility tool surface used for safe bridge/runtime proofing.

tool_pw_navigate.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_click.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_current_page.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_fill.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_playwright_wait.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_playwright_scroll.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_playwright_keypress.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_extract_text.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_extract_links.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_pw_get_elements.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_playwright_extract_links.summary: Legacy Playwright browser-session link extraction alias kept for compatibility.
tool_playwright_extract_links.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

tool_playwright_screenshot.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Playwright browser-session runtime tool that shares page context with other Playwright tools.

command_node.summary: Command-based state update + goto surface. Combines a bounded custom_vars update with direct flow control in one node.
uiAbstractionNotes: Combines a bounded state/custom_vars update with a graph goto in one authored step.
linkSemantics: Direct outgoing edges define the allowed goto targets even though the compiled node returns a Command.
linkMultiplicity: One authored node can both mutate state and jump to a target in the same step.
Direct outgoing edges define allowable goto targets even though the runtime returns a Command rather than following a literal edge callback.
command_node.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Command-returning node with explicit allowed ends; one visual node therefore stands for both state mutation and flow redirection.
debugProjection: state and run surfaces should project both the update payload and the goto target when available

handoff_node.summary: Bounded state-transfer / handoff surface built on Command semantics. Useful for state-driven agent or step transitions without full subagent indirection.
uiAbstractionNotes: Represents a bounded handoff/state-transfer pattern rather than a full autonomous multi-agent runtime.
linkSemantics: Direct outgoing edges define allowed transfer targets while the node updates handoff state.
linkMultiplicity: One authored node can update handoff state and redirect control in the same step.
Outgoing direct edges define legal transfer targets even though the runtime returns a Command.
handoff_node.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a Command-based state-transfer pattern rather than a fully separate autonomous multi-agent runtime.
debugProjection: debug output should distinguish handoff-state updates from ordinary node output

store_put.summary: Runtime store write surface. Persists one selected state value into the current store namespace as a bounded put operation.
linkSemantics: One state input can persist one selected state value into the configured namespace/key.
uiAbstractionNotes: This is a runtime store side-effect node; the graph edge does not expose the namespace/key mechanics directly.
store_put.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store.put/aput side effect plus optional receipt output.
debugProjection: run/debug should identify namespace/key receipts distinctly from ordinary state transitions

store_search.summary: Runtime store search surface. Queries the current store namespace and returns bounded normalized results to the graph state.
linkSemantics: One state input can drive a bounded store search that writes normalized results back into graph state.
uiAbstractionNotes: Search feels like a memory/tool lookup in the UI, but it is a runtime store query side effect under the hood.
store_search.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store.search/asearch call against one namespace derived from params/runtime context.
debugProjection: search results should be surfaced as normalized state outputs, not opaque store internals

send_fanout.summary: Bounded Send API fanout surface. Dispatches one worker payload per source item into a single worker target while keeping the orchestration graph explicit.
uiAbstractionNotes: This node is a graphical dispatch abstraction over LangGraph Send worker creation.
A single outgoing worker edge in the editor can fan out to many worker payloads at runtime.
linkSemantics: One visual edge can represent N worker dispatches.
The compiled graph uses conditional dispatch plus shared-state reduction rather than literal duplicated edges.
linkMultiplicity: A single outgoing worker edge can dispatch many runtime workers.
One source item list can therefore expand into N runtime worker invocations while remaining one authored edge.
send_fanout.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to conditional Send dispatch plus worker-state projection; the canvas intentionally compresses many runtime branches into one worker edge.
debugProjection: fanout worker context is projected into run/debug/state surfaces via fanout_meta
the host graph does not draw one literal edge per runtime worker

store_get.summary: Runtime store read-by-key surface. Retrieves one specific item from the current namespace and normalizes it into graph state.
linkSemantics: One state input can fetch one specific store item by namespace/key.
uiAbstractionNotes: The node looks simple on the canvas even though namespace/key resolution happens in runtime logic.
store_get.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store.get/aget call against one resolved namespace/key.
debugProjection: debug/state should expose the returned item as normalized graph data rather than raw store objects

store_delete.summary: Runtime store delete surface. Removes one item from the current namespace and optionally emits a bounded receipt.
linkSemantics: One state input can delete one specific store item and optionally emit a receipt.
uiAbstractionNotes: Deletion is a side effect on the runtime store; the canvas intentionally keeps that operation compact.
store_delete.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a store.delete/adelete call plus optional normalized receipt payload.
debugProjection: debug/state should identify delete receipts separately from ordinary graph outputs

reduce_join.summary: Bounded reduce/join surface paired with Send API fanout. It summarizes an already-reduced shared state key instead of pretending to be a generic distributed reducer.
linkSemantics: Usually receives one or more worker outputs after a send_fanout dispatch.
Reduces a shared state key rather than mirroring every worker edge literally in the compiled graph.
uiAbstractionNotes: This block is a graphical abstraction over worker-result accumulation plus a final reduction step.
The compiled graph may execute the reduce node multiple times as worker results accumulate; the final state reflects the last reduced value.
linkMultiplicity: One visual inbound path can stand for many worker completions over time.
The reduce node summarizes a shared result key instead of requiring one literal join edge per worker.
reduce_join.compiledGraphRelation: Compiled/runtime behavior reduces shared worker outputs as they accumulate; the authored join node is intentionally simpler than the runtime fanout history.
debugProjection: run/debug/state should show the shared results/progress key rather than pretending every worker path is explicitly drawn

memory_access.summary: Canonical bounded memory access surface for agents/tools. Use this when you want one explicit memory payload output without choosing early between profile lookup, store get, or store search helper variants.
linkSemantics: One state input can resolve into one bounded memory payload output using profile lookup, store get, or store search semantics.
The visual node hides store/profile plumbing behind one canonical memory surface for downstream agent/tool consumption.
uiAbstractionNotes: This is the preferred explicit memory access node for feeding memory payloads into agents or tools via graph handles.
Legacy helper nodes remain available, but this surface is the clearest bounded choice in the current build.
memory_access.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a bounded runtime-store profile/get/search lookup depending on access_mode, normalized into one memory payload output.
debugProjection: debug/state should identify access_mode, namespace, output_key and backend
downstream consumers should treat the output as a normalized memory payload rather than raw store internals

subgraph_node.summary: Child subgraph surface or saved subgraph reference. Distinct from LangChain-derived subagents and used for graph-native child editing/composition.
uiAbstractionNotes: This block is the graph-native child/subgraph surface. It represents either an editable child subgraph tab or a saved subgraph reference.
Use this instead of sub_agent when the intent is graph composition rather than a LangChain-derived agent artifact.
linkSemantics: One visual node can stand for an editable child subgraph or a saved subgraph artifact reference.
Its direct edges are authored as ordinary graph links even when the compiled/runtime target is a nested graph.
linkMultiplicity: The canvas node represents one child graph reference, not every internal node of that child graph.
Its direct edges remain ordinary authoring links while the compiled graph invokes a nested graph.
subgraph_node.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a nested graph/subgraph call on the trunk. The child editor tab is a product abstraction, not a second public runtime.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should identify the child graph or saved subgraph source
reopen flow points to the child subgraph editor or saved subgraph artifact

runtime_context_read.summary: Reads static runtime context passed at invoke/stream time and projects it into graph state.
uiAbstractionNotes: Reads static runtime context, not mutable graph state. Use runtime settings to provide values at run time.
runtime_context_read.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a graph-native node that reads Runtime.context and emits a value or payload into state.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should show context reads separately from state updates

structured_output_extract.summary: Extracts one field or the full payload from a structured output object already present in state/custom_vars.
uiAbstractionNotes: Use after an LLM/agent configured with structured output to project specific fields into simpler graph keys.
structured_output_extract.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a graph-native node that reads structured output from state/custom_vars and emits an extracted value.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should show source structured key and extracted field

structured_output_router.summary: Routes execution based on a field inside a structured output payload.
uiAbstractionNotes: Acts like a logic router but reads a field from structured output rather than free-form text/JSON.
structured_output_router.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to a conditional router function over a structured output field.
debugProjection: runtime/debug surfaces should identify the structured field used for routing

tool_tavily_extract.summary: Tavily provider-backed URL extraction tool surface.
tool_tavily_extract.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the Tavily-backed tavily_extract runtime tool.

tool_sql_list_tables.summary: SQL inspection surface for listing tables.
tool_sql_list_tables.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the sql_list_tables runtime tool.

tool_sql_get_schema.summary: SQL inspection surface for reading table schema.
tool_sql_get_schema.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the sql_get_schema runtime tool.

tool_sql_query_check.summary: SQL validation surface for checking read-only queries before execution.
tool_sql_query_check.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the sql_query_check runtime tool.

tool_github_get_issue.summary: GitHub provider-backed read surface for one issue.
tool_github_get_issue.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the github_get_issue runtime tool.

tool_github_get_pull_request.summary: GitHub provider-backed read surface for one pull request.
tool_github_get_pull_request.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the github_get_pull_request runtime tool.

tool_github_read_file.summary: GitHub provider-backed read surface for repository files.
tool_github_read_file.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the github_read_file runtime tool.

tool_github_search_issues_prs.summary: GitHub provider-backed read surface for issues and pull requests search.
tool_github_search_issues_prs.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the github_search_issues_prs runtime tool.

tool_brave_search.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the Brave-backed brave_search runtime tool.

tool_duckduckgo_search.summary: DuckDuckGo search tool surface backed by the duckduckgo-search package.
tool_duckduckgo_search.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the duckduckgo_search runtime tool.

tool_requests_get.summary: Requests toolkit GET surface for stateless HTTP reads.
tool_requests_get.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the requests_get runtime tool with author-configured HTTP scope.

tool_requests_post.summary: Requests toolkit POST surface for stateless HTTP writes or RPC-style calls.
tool_requests_post.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the requests_post runtime tool with author-configured HTTP scope.

tool_fs_list_dir.summary: Read-only local filesystem listing from a bounded root path.
tool_fs_list_dir.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_list_dir runtime tool with a bounded local root path.

tool_fs_read_file.summary: Read one local text file from a bounded root path.
tool_fs_read_file.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_read_file runtime tool with bounded local file access.

tool_fs_glob.summary: Read-only glob search across a bounded local filesystem root.
tool_fs_glob.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_glob runtime tool with bounded local root search.

tool_fs_grep.summary: Read-only grep-style text search across a bounded local filesystem root.
tool_fs_grep.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_grep runtime tool with bounded local root text search.

tool_fs_write_file.summary: Preview or apply one local text-file write under a bounded root path with explicit create-vs-overwrite and size guards.
tool_fs_write_file.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_write_file runtime tool with explicit mode='preview'|'apply'; preview reports create-vs-overwrite without mutating, and apply performs the bounded local write.
debugProjection: During runs, graph and logs should distinguish preview from apply. Preview is advisory and must not mutate the filesystem.
Run/debug surfaces should expose create-vs-overwrite, bounded target path, and explicit blocked reasons such as root escape or overwrite denial.
A node may look identical on the authored graph while the run history records whether the last execution was preview, applied, blocked, or failed.
uiAbstractionNotes: One authored tool node represents a bounded local write capability; the mutation only happens when the tool is invoked in apply mode.
linkSemantics: Links expose the write capability to the receiving step; they do not imply that a file will be mutated on every run.

tool_fs_edit_file.summary: Preview or apply one local text-file edit under a bounded root path with explicit match-count and size guards.
tool_fs_edit_file.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_edit_file runtime tool with explicit mode='preview'|'apply'; preview reports match counts without mutating, and apply performs the bounded local edit.
debugProjection: During runs, graph and logs should distinguish preview from apply. Preview reports match counts and replacement intent without mutating.
Run/debug surfaces should expose the bounded target path, match count when known, replace-all intent, and explicit blocked reasons such as missing target or no match.
A node may look identical on the authored graph while the run history records whether the last execution was preview, applied, blocked, or failed.
uiAbstractionNotes: One authored tool node represents a bounded local edit capability; the edit only happens when the tool is invoked in apply mode.
linkSemantics: Links expose the edit capability to the receiving step; they do not imply that text replacement will occur on every run.

tool_shell_command.summary: User-armed bounded local shell subprocess surface with cwd and command allowlist guards. Returns explicit blocked/failed/succeeded statuses. Not OS-container isolated.
tool_shell_command.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the shell_command runtime tool and stays blocked unless the graph runtime shell toggle is armed.
debugProjection: Run/debug surfaces should expose whether shell was blocked because the runtime toggle was off, the cwd was invalid, or the command was outside the allowlist.
This surface is user-armed and root-bounded, not sandboxed. The graph should not imply isolated execution or dry-run support.
A node may look identical on the authored graph while the run history records blocked, failed, or succeeded shell execution.
uiAbstractionNotes: One authored tool node represents bounded shell capability gated by runtime settings and command allowlists.
linkSemantics: Links expose shell capability to the receiving step; they do not imply that shell execution is armed for the current run.

tool_fs_apply_patch.summary: Preview or apply a bounded unified diff patch under a local root path with explicit touched-file, creation, rejection, and size guards.
tool_fs_apply_patch.compiledGraphRelation: Compiles to the fs_apply_patch runtime tool with explicit mode='preview'|'apply'; preview parses and validates touched files without mutating, and apply writes only when the bounded patch plan is not blocked.
debugProjection: During runs, graph and logs should distinguish preview from apply. Preview parses and validates a bounded patch plan without mutating.
Run/debug surfaces should expose files to modify, files to create, files rejected, and explicit blocked reasons such as invalid patch or root escape.
Apply may still be non-atomic across multiple files; the runtime should report applied, blocked, failed, or partially applied truthfully rather than implying full atomicity.
uiAbstractionNotes: One authored tool node represents a bounded local patch capability; the graph stays compact even when one patch touches several files.
linkSemantics: Links expose the patch capability to the receiving step; they do not imply that every touched file is already accepted or writable.

composition.label: Rail 1 · Structural composition
composition.description: Subgraphs, wrappers, references, imported graph-like units, and reusable composition surfaces.

agentic.label: Rail 2 · Agentic / assistant
agentic.description: Agent-flavored shells that still depend on the trunk but now have explicit bounded meaning.

adapter.label: Rail 3 · Adapter / future-runtime
adapter.description: Adapter-backed or alias-backed surfaces that are broader than the trunk but not equal to it.

services.label: Rail 4 · Platform services
services.description: Artifact library, capability matrix, packaging, validation, and coordination services.

interoperabilityBridges.summary: Saved LangGraph subgraphs can be reopened or inserted as wrapper-backed child graph references.

Bounded LangChain agent artifacts can be lowered into LangGraph wrapper-backed subgraphs when they stay within explicit bridge contracts. Minimal agents use v1; small shared-safe tool subsets use v2; unsupported shapes remain editor/package-only.
bridgeConstraints: v1: no custom tools, no nested artifact wrappers, react_agent nodes lower to llm_chat, all other nodes must already be LangGraph-allowed shared nodes
v2: only one shared-safe tool family per artifact is allowed
v2: allowed tool families are rpg_dice_roller or read-only sql_query
v2: tool-linked nodes must be react_agent and linked tools must exist in the artifact tool list
nested artifact wrapper bridges remain unsupported
interoperabilityBridges.bridgeAcceptedSourceShape: LangChain agent artifacts using react_agent plus LangGraph-safe shared nodes; tool-enabled variants may link exactly one shared-safe tool family to react_agent.

interoperabilityBridges.summary: A bounded LangChain agent artifact can be embedded as a native authored artifact node inside LangGraph orchestration. Provider-free and one bounded OpenAI-backed shape are supported; lowered bridges remain a separate integration model.
bridgeConstraints: accepted shape is one bounded LangChain agent component using react_agent, llm_chat, or shared-safe local nodes
provider-backed embedded execution is currently bounded to OpenAI react_agent/llm_chat shapes using gpt-4o* models
shared-safe tools remain limited to one allowed family linked through react_agent
nested artifact wrapper bridges remain unsupported
embedded-native execution preserves artifact identity and wrapper reopen flow
interoperabilityBridges.bridgeAcceptedSourceShape: LangChain agent artifacts kept in native authored form with one bounded component using react_agent, llm_chat, or shared-safe local nodes; shared-safe tools remain limited to exactly one allowed family linked through react_agent.

interoperabilityBridges.summary: DeepAgents artifacts can be inserted into LangGraph workspaces as wrapper references for packaging/editor coordination only.

trunk_runtime.label: Trunk runtime
trunk_runtime.description: Compiles and runs on the real LangGraph trunk without relying on an alias surface or an adapter-only bridge.

bridge_backed_runtime.label: Bridge-backed runtime
bridge_backed_runtime.description: Runs through a real runtime path, but only through a wrapper, bridge, embedded-native reference, or other adapter-backed surface.

editor_only.label: Editor-only
editor_only.description: Visible for authoring, packaging, compatibility, or inspection, but not exposed as an independently runnable runtime surface in the current build.

alias_backed.label: Alias-backed
alias_backed.description: Looks like its own surface in the editor, but currently compiles into another canonical runtime node or trunk path.
